event_blocks.summary: An upbeat, quirky tune accompanies the opening title card, 'Abbott Elementary: Principal Ava’s Most Unhinged Moments Part 1,' before transitioning to the exterior of Willard R. Abbott Elementary. Principal Ava, in a white coat, enthusiastically greets staff members, announcing the district's approval of an emergency budget and the immediate release of funds. When Janine suggests using the money for rugs, Ava dismisses the idea, prioritizing something more immediate and comparing her own need for a good hairstyle to fixing the 'outside' to improve the 'inside.' The scene shifts to a library or meeting room where Ava reveals she spent the entire $3,000 from the district on a plastic sign, prompting disbelief from Melissa and a sarcastic remark from Gregory. Mr. Johnson questions their purpose, to which Ava vaguely replies, 'Well, chocolate drop.' Later, in a well-lit library with educational posters, Ava confronts staff about a perceived lack of respect for her and the school, proposing an exercise where they 'say whatever we want out loud,' starting with Janine, whom Ava describes as 'pushy, squeaky, and annoying.' After a male staff member reluctantly participates, Ava calls in a young boy, Sheena, who is hungry and holding a stuffed animal, to continue the exercise. The scene then focuses on Janine apologizing for disrespecting Ava and causing the staff to miss lunch, explaining she emailed the superintendent about Ava spending school money on the sign and emphasizing her concern for the children. A woman with short black hair then criticizes Janine as a 'charisma vacuum' but concedes she is 'right' about wanting to help the children.

Mural.description: A large wall mural featuring a close-up of a child’s face with the word 'ACHIEVE' repeated vertically in blue and yellow text, visible in the background during the presentation.

Willard R. Abbott Elementary Sign.description: A large green sign with white cursive text reading 'Willard R. Abbott Elementary' and a stylized image of a woman in a blue suit posing with one hand on her hip, which Principal Ava refers to as 'a plastic sign' and the object of her spending the emergency budget.

Staff Members.description: A diverse group of adults, mostly women, wearing professional outerwear and lanyards with ID badges; they display a range of reactions from amusement to surprise, standing or sitting in the library/meeting room, some holding food or notebooks, and are heard discussing and reacting to Janine's speech, with some offering critical commentary.

Library/Meeting Room.description: An indoor setting with bookshelves, tables, and chairs, where staff members are seated, some holding food, indicating a casual or informal meeting environment; posters like 'Dream BIG' and a mural with 'ACHIEVE' are visible on the walls.

Principal Ava.description: A woman with long blonde hair, wearing a white coat over a patterned top, a lanyard with an ID badge, and a brown skirt; she is expressive and animated, frequently gesturing while speaking in a clear, assertive, and engaging female voice. She leads a discussion, proposes an exercise, clarifies its constructive intent, and interacts warmly with a young boy named Sheena, whose name she confirms.

Young Boy in School Uniform.description: A child with curly hair and glasses, wearing a light blue school polo shirt with a circular emblem on the chest, identified by the speaker as 'Sheena.' He stands near the front, holding a stuffed animal, and is offered a plate with a sandwich, which he politely declines with a young voice.

Woman with Short Black Hair.description: A woman with short black hair, wearing a patterned blazer over a purple top and layered necklaces, who speaks with a serious, critical expression. She comments on Janine's speaking style, calling her a 'charisma vacuum' and describing her as 'naive, a bit clingy, too cheerful,' while also acknowledging Janine's sincerity in wanting to help the children.

Woman in Blue Floral Blouse.description: A woman with curly hair, wearing a blue floral blouse and a lanyard, identified as 'Janine.' She speaks animatedly and gestures while leading a discussion, apologizing for disrespecting 'Ava' and causing staff to miss lunch, and justifying her actions by stating she cares about the kids in the school. Her speech is later critically analyzed by the Woman with Short Black Hair.

raw_segments: The video opens with an upbeat, quirky background tune accompanying the title card, 'Abbott Elementary: Principal Ava’s Most Unhinged Moments Part 1.' The scene transitions to the exterior of Willard R. Abbott Elementary, where Principal Ava, an animated woman in a white coat, enthusiastically greets staff members with "Good morning!" Her staff, including Gregory, respond before Ava proudly announces that "The district was so moved by my plea that they approved the emergency budget and sent us the money right away," eliciting surprised "Oh!"s and "Hey!"s from the group. She explains they could have hired aides or gotten rugs, but she wanted "something more immediate." Janine, a staff member, interjects that "the rugs are immediate," like "instant Xanax for kids," having explained it in an email, which Jacob questions. Ava then shifts topics, stating she always feels better with her hair done, leading to better work, and that fixing the "outside" makes the "inside take care of itself," accompanied by a whoosh sound effect. The camera pans to a close-up of the school’s green sign, featuring the name 'Willard R. Abbott Elementary' and a stylized woman. Inside a library or meeting room, with staff seated around a table, Ava asks, "Y'all seeing this? A plastic sign." She reveals, "Thank God for the school district 'cause they gave us $3,000 and I had to spend all of it" on the sign. Melissa exclaims in disbelief, "You spent all of the money on this?" Ava proudly defends it as a "Rush job. Can you believe its quality?" Gregory sarcastically asks, "How's that optimism taste?" before Mr. Johnson questions, "Why are we here exactly?" to which Ava dismissively replies, "Well, chocolate drop." The quirky background music continues throughout the lively and comedic exchanges.

The video opens in a well-lit library or meeting room, adorned with 'Dream BIG' and 'ACHIEVE' posters, where a diverse group of staff members, mostly women in professional attire and ID badges, are seated and standing. Principal Ava, a woman with long blonde hair in a white coat and patterned top, animatedly leads the discussion, her clear, assertive female voice declaring, "I learned that someone here doesn't respect me... how can you respect this school?" Her lively expressions and gestures engage the attentive staff, whose reactions range from amusement to surprise. A male staff member's questioning voice asks, "Who doesn't respect you, Ava?" to which she responds, "It's not important. We're going to make this a group matter." She then proposes an exercise, inviting them to "say whatever we want out loud," and addresses a female staff member, "Let's start with Janine." Janine, in a soft, hesitant voice, replies, "Yes," only for Principal Ava to describe her as "pushy, squeaky, and annoying," prompting Janine's surprised "Excuse me?" and another male staff member's interjection. Ava quickly clarifies the "constructive" nature of the exercise before calling on "Hershey Kiss," a male staff member who reluctantly states, "I really don't want to." As Ava tries to involve "Jacob, Barbara," and another staff member interjects, she firmly takes control, stating, "No one's doing this to anyone. Sheena, come on in." A female staff member expresses concern, "Ava, that is my student. She should be at lunch," as a young boy with curly hair and glasses, wearing a light blue school polo, approaches. He speaks in a soft, slightly whiny child's voice, confirming, "I am kind of hungry," and Principal Ava places a hand on his shoulder, gently asking, "Sheena, remember what we talked about?" reinforcing the warm, community-focused atmosphere.

The scene unfolds in a bright library or meeting room, where a group of staff members are gathered, some seated and others standing, creating a semi-casual yet tense atmosphere. A male voice initiates a discussion, asking what they wish was different about 'Miss Teagues,' prompting another male voice to humorously remark on her 'big feet.' The woman in a blue floral blouse, identified by the audio as Janine, then takes the floor, speaking animatedly and gesturing as she apologizes for disrespecting 'Ava' and causing everyone to miss lunch. She specifically addresses a young boy, identified as Sheena, who stands near the front in a light blue school polo, holding a stuffed animal, and politely declines a sandwich being offered to him. Janine justifies her actions, stating she emailed the superintendent about Ava spending school money on a sign, emphasizing her care for the children in the school. As she concludes, the camera pans across the room, capturing various staff members’ reactions, from attentive to surprised or concerned. Following the sound of footsteps, a woman with short black hair, wearing a patterned blazer, speaks with a serious, critical tone, labeling Janine a 'charisma vacuum' and describing her as 'naive, a bit clingy, too cheerful,' though she ultimately concedes Janine is 'right' about wanting to help the children at the school. In the background, posters like 'Dream BIG' and a mural with 'ACHIEVE' are visible, reinforcing the educational environment amidst the engaged, possibly tense, professional interaction.

event_blocks.summary: In a library, staff members, including Ava in a blue floral blouse, discuss an impending event, with Ava dramatically proclaiming 'The end times!' The scene shifts to a dimly lit basement where a woman with a headlamp searches shelves, and others, including a woman in a green sleeveless top, express concern. Ava, on a ladder with a flashlight, inspects the ceiling, prompting a warning not to shake the ladder. A male voice states Gregory is the only one allowed in his bunker, while the woman in the green sleeveless top identifies as a doomsday prepper, referencing zombie apocalypse scenarios. Ava clarifies it's a partial power outage and directs everyone without power to the gym for classes. A woman with short black hair apologizes for causing a delay, and another female voice remarks on the outcome. Later, in the basement, a male voice directs everyone to the gym due to 'bigger fish to fry.' Amidst the chaos, a frantic male voice exclaims about his 'Branzino,' as Janine, the Woman in Beige Turtleneck, appears startled and feels lightheaded, having missed breakfast. The Man in Pink Shirt crouches beside the Person on Floor, illuminated by a flashlight, as a female voice obtains consent to slap the person, who then passes out, eliciting a gasp and a humorous remark about zombies from the Woman in Blue Floral Blouse. The scene transitions to a brightly lit library where Janine questions why staff are looking at her, and the Woman with Short Black Hair reveals Janine has only dated one man her entire life. Janine, tense, asks about other women, and a male voice comments it explains much. The Woman in Blue Floral Blouse demands a chair amidst laughter. Janine asks if the man is rich, and the Woman with Short Black Hair describes him as an aspiring musician, not monetarily wealthy, but 'sweet, caring, ambitious,' referring to him as Tariq.

He's sweet, he's caring, he's ambitious.'

raw_segments: The segment opens in a bustling library or meeting room where staff members are gathered, some seated with food, while others stand. A woman with curly hair, wearing a blue floral blouse and a lanyard, identified as Ava, speaks animatedly, stating something 'be a bad thing' before asking 'where is everybody going?' A male voice responds that they are 'to check on Janine,' followed by another male voice adding 'to eat lunch, after I check on Janine.' A calm male voice advises everyone to 'stay calm,' but Ava excitedly interrupts, declaring, 'This is it, y'all! The end times!' and exclaiming 'it's happening!' The scene abruptly shifts to a dimly lit basement or storage area. Here, a woman with a headlamp searches cluttered shelves, while others, including a woman in a green sleeveless top, look concerned. A female voice shouts, 'Don't shake the ladder!' as Ava, now on a ladder and inspecting the ceiling with a flashlight, is watched intently by the group. A male voice states, 'Gregory is the only person that can stay in my bunker, so stop asking.' The woman in the green sleeveless top, with a distinct voice, then asserts, 'Hell yeah, I'm a doomsday prepper,' questioning why more people aren't and referencing 'Train to Busan' and 'fast zombies,' adding, 'That day is coming. That day soon comes.' Ava, from the ladder, calmly clarifies, 'Ava, it is just a partial power outage,' then instructs everyone to 'listen up!' A male voice echoes, 'Listen up, y'all!' and a female voice asks, 'Are you kidding me?' Ava continues, explaining the plan: 'everybody without power, please, head to the gym. We will conduct classes there until this is all fixed. It is not ideal, but it will work.' A male voice urges, 'You heard her, let's go!' Finally, a woman with short black hair, wearing a patterned blazer, apologetically states, 'I'm sorry everybody, I just thought if I could get up here and get this done, we wouldn't have to wait,' to which another female voice responds, 'And look where it landed us, baby girl.'

The minute opens in a dimly lit basement where staff, including the Woman in Green Sleeveless Top and the Woman in Striped Shirt with Headlamp, are investigating. A male voice directs everyone to the gym, stating, "we've got bigger fish to fry now." Amidst the urgency, a frantic male voice exclaims, "Oh Jesus, my Branzino! Everybody out of the way!" as the Woman in Beige Turtleneck, later identified as Janine, appears startled and gestures, admitting, "I just... I feel lightheaded." A concerned female voice asks if she's eaten, and another male voice adds she missed breakfast. The scene cuts to the Man in Pink Shirt crouched beside the Person on Floor, illuminated by a flashlight, as a female voice urgently declares, "Okay, we're losing her. Do I have your consent to slap you?" followed by "There she goes! She's out!" and a collective gasp, confirming the person has passed out. The Woman in Blue Floral Blouse humorously reacts, "Oh my god! She pale like a zombie! You know they eat the hottest people first. Let me back my tasty ass up." The setting then transitions to a brightly lit library where staff are eating and conversing. Janine (Woman in Beige Turtleneck) asks, "Why are you all looking at me like that?" The Woman with Short Black Hair then addresses Janine directly, revealing, "Janine, sweetheart, you have only dated one man your entire life." Janine, standing with a tense expression, asks, "No women? Nobody else ever?" A male voice comments, "That just answers so many things." The Woman in Blue Floral Blouse animatedly demands, "Somebody give me a chair!" amidst laughter. Janine then asks, "Is... is the guy rich?" to which the Woman with Short Black Hair replies, "No, not monetarily. He's an aspiring musician." The Woman in Blue Floral Blouse interjects, "So he broke. Somebody give me a fresh chair!" The Woman with Short Black Hair defends him, saying, "Look, you don't know Tariq. He's sweet, he's caring, he's ambitious." On-screen text identifies the episode as 'Work Family,' Season 1, Episode 8.

event_blocks.summary: In a library/meeting room, Barbara, the Woman in Blue Floral Blouse, animatedly tells Jacob, the Man in Dark Blazer, that 'Jacob knows' and playfully suggests he needs 'a little bit more help than that,' to which Jacob, displaying a 'Willard R. Abbott Elementary' lanyard, describes himself as 'very spirited,' 'confident,' and a 'real extemporaneous thinker.' Barbara continues to question their friendship, asking if he's 'secretly Mormon' after he beatboxes and says 'one joystick,' prompting Jacob's humorous retort, 'No. Oh, you should be 'cause you need more man,' before Barbara calls out her own name. The Woman with Short Black Hair compliments Janine, the Woman in Beige Turtleneck, on her cleverness, to which Janine, startled, urgently advises, 'Get all your desks to the gym until this whole thing blows over,' exclaiming, 'life is a joke, people.' The scene transitions to a hallway with brick walls, where Jacob urges haste, asking, 'Come on, hurry up. Who knows when those kids are start robbing?' The Woman in Black Lace Top, leading a discussion, states the children arrive at 7:30, prompting surprise from the Woman in Green Sleeveless Top who asks, 'Every day? That's wild.' The Woman in Black Lace Top then demands to see 'these permission slips to the zoo,' warning of her ability to detect fakes, specifically mentioning 'Herbie Hancock,' which the Woman in Green Sleeveless Top corrects to 'John Hancock.' The segment concludes with a smiling Janine guiding children in school uniforms through the hallway, holding papers.

event_blocks.summary: In a school hallway with beige brick walls, the Woman in Beige Turtleneck guides uniformed children, while the Man in Mustard Shirt declares his intention to speak freely, prompting the Woman in Beige Turtleneck to express concern over potential "mishaps" and unfiled paperwork. When asked why they are proceeding, the Man in Mustard Shirt reveals a desire to visit the zoo. The scene shifts to a classroom where Principal Ava, wearing a yellow and brown striped dress, kindly gives the Young Boy in School Uniform, Micah, a sandwich. The Man in Grey Blazer announces Micah's return from his office, and the Man in Plaid Shirt prompts Micah for a statement to the class, to which Micah responds, "Not really." After the Man in Plaid Shirt inquires what he should say, the Man in Grey Blazer demands an apology and a promise of future good behavior from Micah. The Man in Plaid Shirt sarcastically predicts future disruptions and questions the request for a lie, to which the Man in Grey Blazer agrees. The Man in Plaid Shirt then reminds Micah of his "actual lotto" and poses a riddle about an axolotl on vacation, which Micah answers with the pun "Relaxolotl." The setting transitions to a bright staff lounge, identified as 'Principal's Office,' where the Man in Grey Blazer, now in a grey cardigan over a mustard shirt, animatedly complains to the Man in Plaid Shirt about Principal Ava making him "take candy from a baby" and not disciplining Micah. The Man in Plaid Shirt counters that "Ava doesn't discipline the kids," and when the Man in Grey Blazer questions why he was directed to send them to her, the Man in Plaid Shirt explains it's to regain classroom control. The Man in Grey Blazer argues this rewards bad behavior, as other staff members, including the Woman in Green Sleeveless Top and the Woman with Red Hair, observe the casual, collaborative atmosphere amidst visible kitchen appliances and vending machines.

event_blocks.summary: Outside the Brick School Building Exterior, Children in School Uniforms, some holding Boxes of TRUMBULL PENS & PENCILS for a fundraiser, are addressed by the Woman in Grey Coat with Fur Collar, identified as Principal Coleman, who asserts she'll "take the lead today" with "new tactics," warning them not to forget her teachings. A Man in Brown Jacket and Flat Cap interjects, advising them to "forget everything she taught you" and to "act like you've already made a sale," singling out "Courtney." Principal Coleman questions the man about the number of candy bars he'd purchase for "this adorable child," to which he reluctantly agrees to one, asking for change. Principal Coleman sarcastically retorts that "change is not gonna come." The Young Girl in Brown Coat cheerfully states, "Sorry, we don't have change," followed by a "yoink" sound. As the camera follows the girl past a yellow 'SLOW WATCH FOR CHILDREN' Sign, Principal Coleman blows a red whistle, startling the children and the Woman in Black Leather Coat, identified as Barb, who holds up her phone in surprise. Principal Coleman advises the children to feign illness, suggesting "polio," to elicit sympathy and sales, adding, "And you're gonna wanna look out for liberal white people." The man identifies as "progressive," prompting the young girl to ask if there's a difference. Later, the Man in Brown Jacket and Flat Cap discusses sales tactics, stating, "You'll be able to spot them because they'll be making this face," and adding, "If you can't sell candy bars to them, you're unteachable." A child's voice exclaims "Ow!" prompting Barbara to ask, "Malika, are you okay?" Malika responds, "No, but I'd feel a lot better if you bought some candy from me," and then attempts a manipulative sales pitch, "An at-risk black youth." Barbara blows a red whistle to manage the children, then sternly addresses Ava, accusing her of turning students into "scammers" with "aggressive" and "predatory" tactics, likening it to a "check-cashing store." Ava defends her methods, stating, "This is how you break through the noise." Barbara sets firm conditions: "No lying, and no racial profiling!"

raw_segments: The video opens in a brightly lit classroom, featuring colorful rugs, desks, and a large 'PENMANSHIP' chart. A woman in a maroon cardigan, white blouse, and patterned scarf (the Woman in Maroon Dress with Patterned Scarf) stands animatedly, gesturing while speaking in an instructional tone about "communal seating" being "better for kindergartners' development." She addresses another woman, the Woman in Black 'HARVARD' Sweatshirt, who holds a clipboard, wears glasses and a silver necklace, and listens with a mix of defiance and amusement, responding in a dry, slightly sarcastic tone that she prefers her "desk separated" for ease of movement. The Woman in Maroon Dress then adopts an exaggerated, high-pitched, mocking voice, mimicking a child named "Little Jonathan" who "never learned how to make direct eye contact" and "can't hold down a job," to which the Woman in Black 'HARVARD' Sweatshirt dryly agrees to "take that into account." The Woman in Maroon Dress firmly declares it a "District mandate" that "cursive is no longer in the curriculum," followed by a sound of papers being thrown or a desk being hit. The scene then cuts to an office with large windows, a desk cluttered with binders and a potted plant, and a modern, professional feel. A woman in a striped shirt with red and black vertical lines (the Woman in Striped Shirt, later identified as Ava) enters, holding papers, muttering "Damn it" in frustration. She confronts another woman, Janine (the Woman in Black V-Neck Blouse), who is seated at the desk wearing a black blazer over a white V-neck top. Janine calmly greets Ava, who then exclaims, "No! What the hell?" Janine, with a disapproving and firm tone, expresses disbelief that Ava has "a picture of Gregory in your desk drawer," prompting Ava to retort sarcastically. Janine then directly asks what Ava is doing there, to which Ava assertively states she's "here with a demand." Janine corrects her, suggesting she means "a request," but Ava insists she needs "a company card." After a brief back-and-forth clarifying if she means "card" or "car," and Janine's blunt refusal, Ava explains her urgency in trying to get "an ASL translator for Jacob's class." Janine, now agreeable, offers to hire one and put it "on the company card," but Ava clarifies that Janine is "not getting a company card," leaving Janine exasperated and trailing off, questioning the point of Ava being there. On-screen text identifies the segment as 'Gregory's Garden Goofballs,' Season 3, Episode 3.

The video opens in an office setting where the Woman in Striped Shirt, wearing a white shirt with red and black vertical stripes, is seated at a desk, holding papers and speaking with urgency, her voice heard denying she's a 'mole' and asserting her importance. A male voice interjects, calling her 'more like a pimple.' The Woman in Black V-Neck Blouse, dressed in a black blazer over a white V-neck top, stands across from her, reacting with a stern expression and stating she's trying to help a student. The male voice then announces he's going to get 'free fancy bubbly waters,' which the Woman in Black V-Neck Blouse challenges, asking if the clear bottle labeled 'SANDY DO NOT DRINK' she holds was taken from the communal fridge, to which the male voice claims he brought it from home. The scene shifts to a school hallway where the Woman in Black V-Neck Blouse, now wearing a navy blue dress with a lanyard, walks briskly while holding a phone and a beige coat, her concerned expression matching the sounds of footsteps and distant chatter. She passes a Young Boy in School Uniform sitting on a wooden bench with a blue backpack, who looks up at her. As she rushes down the hallway, joined by a group of staff members including the Woman in Green Turtleneck, the Man in Plaid Shirt, and the Woman with Red Hair, all displaying expressions of alarm, a woman's excited voice exclaims, 'I just saw a ghost y'all!' followed by a cacophony of excited, overlapping voices discussing the 'ghosts' multiplying. They converge at a reception desk where the Man in Grey Blazer, whose calm voice is heard introducing himself and his son Charlie, states they are looking for Principal Coleman and that it's Charlie's first day, having enrolled via the district website. The Woman in Beige Blazer stands with him, holding the Young Boy. Amidst the panic, a woman's voice urgently asks if others see 'them too,' and another jokingly suggests getting a 'proton pack' before being told to 'stop.' The scene cuts to a different hallway where the Woman in Brown Turtleneck, wearing a brown top with green and white trim and a lanyard, speaks with urgency, her voice explaining that a big-time PGA golf course is being built about ten blocks away, which is 'allegedly good for the city' and popular. The final shot transitions to an outdoor construction site, showing a yellow CAT skid-steer loader moving dirt near a large circular sand pit, with workers and a 'WATER RENOVATION' sign visible in the background, indicating ongoing school grounds improvements.

The video opens with the Woman in Brown Turtleneck standing in a school hallway near a staircase, speaking animatedly and gesturing, her expression shifting from serious to smiling, as she discusses new people moving to West Philly and the demographics of golfers. She wears a brown top with green and white trim and a lanyard with 'Willard R. Abbott Elementary' visible. The scene transitions to a group of staff members and a Young Boy in School Uniform, identified as Charlie, gathered near a reception desk in a warmly lit hallway. Among them, the Man in Grey Blazer expresses excitement to be there, while the Woman in Beige Turtleneck, who identifies herself as Miss Teagues, speaks quickly and enthusiastically about choosing public education, followed by laughter. The Man in Grey Blazer welcomes them, and another woman's voice notes, "This is new, huh?" The Man in Grey Blazer adds, "First time in all my years," and expresses being "thrilled to meet you" and having a new student. Miss Teagues then warmly welcomes Charlie, stating she is his teacher. The Woman in Brown Turtleneck joins the group, smiling and engaging. The Woman in Black V-Neck Blouse, now in a navy dress, walks briskly through the hallway with a concerned expression, holding a phone and a beige coat, and identifies herself with a deep, authoritative voice as Principal Coleman, warmly welcoming Charlie to the "rich, diverse tapestry of Abbott Elementary." The group appears cheerful and collaborative. The scene shifts to a staff lounge where the Woman in Green Sleeveless Top is seated, pointing and speaking animatedly, while a Man in Teal Top stands nearby holding a red cloth. On-screen text identifies the episode as 'Ringworm' from Season 4, Episode 2. The atmosphere transitions from a formal hallway to a more casual staff lounge, with natural lighting and kitchen appliances visible. The Man in Grey Blazer then mentions, "We being audited. Ringworm," followed by Miss Teagues calling out, "Jacob, come here," and the Man in Grey Blazer interjecting, "Nope, get back."

event_blocks.summary: In a staff lounge, a man in a striped sweater questions a woman with red hair about touching something; she explains she sent someone home for ringworm and sanitized surfaces, prompting others, including a woman in a striped shirt and a woman in a maroon dress with an orange, to exclaim, "That's not enough!" The woman in the striped shirt warns that "Jacob, ringworm is in elementary school's worst enemy," before a male voice counts "One, two, three" and expresses relief. Outdoors, a MAX Express van is parked near a 'MOTIVATION' mural, and a woman in a blue top unloads delivery boxes, handing one to a surprised woman in a purple cardigan with pearls, identified as Ava. As a man in a brown jacket approaches and a vehicle screeches, the woman in the blue top shouts to "Bring 'em inside!" and criticizes the "Zero stars!" delivery, instructing the others to "lift with your legs, brace your back" due to lack of insurance. Ava retorts that her back doesn't brace and asks Barb (the woman in the blue top) for the young man's phone number, to which Barb replies she'll "dial 411 before I pick up." A male voice humorously comments about "Jesus" being lean and muscular. Inside, a woman in a green dress enters an office setting with a serious expression, where a man in a dark jacket and glasses, holding a metal cup, asks, "Who you talking to?" and then, "You. What do you want?" He continues, stating, "I know there wasn't a lost ticket and I know that the district..." before the audio cuts off. Later, in Principal Ava’s office, she, wearing a mustard yellow blouse and ID badge, is seated at her desk, in a tense conversation with the Man in Dark Jacket, who stands across from her. He firmly states, "not give you these computers." Principal Ava, gesturing, frustratedly asks, "Damn it. Who gave it away? Barb?" The Man in Dark Jacket reveals, "It was Principal Coleman." Ava, surprised, defensively retorts, "Well, that can't be right. That's me." He leans forward, explaining the computers' advanced processor confirmed his suspicions and accusatorily asks, "Where did you get these?" Ava scoffs, defiantly asking, "You think I got in this chair by folding under pressure?" The Man in Dark Jacket warns that if she doesn't tell him, he'll assume they're stolen and report it. After a pause, Ava explains, "We got the rich people building the golf course to donate them." He questions, "You got them to?" Ava, exasperated, admits, "Fine. It was blackmail, black man, a bribe, quid pro quo!" The camera alternates between close-ups and medium shots, emphasizing their emotional engagement in the professionally lit office.
